raw_consumer_statement: Started with receipt of credit card on XX/XX/XXXX under account ending XXXX. Card/account had been paid off as of XX/XX/XXXX. I did not activate the card but on XX/XX/XXXX I received an email from XXXX XXXX saying I owed them {$54.00}. XXXX and I are in the process of leaving this timeshare outfit, which we began this process in XX/XX/XXXX. I contacted Barclayscard and asked for a credit to card ending XXXX, which they did give me. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, I received word that it was determined not a fraud as we received a reward in points. Now, an account ending in XXXX shows up with a charge of {$54.00}. I then contacted XXXX XXXX  and explained all this. XXXX ', a XXXX XXXX XXXX employee removed an auto-renewal and hopefully I would receive a credit within 5-7 business days. It did not happen. Payment of {$54.00} was due on XX/XX/XXXX. Account ending XXXX (? ) with Barclayscard still shows {$54.00} due. I have tried to call various XXXX numbers to speak with an account rep. I have been hung up on and a " chat '' bot, after explaining all this again, wanted me to pay {$5.00} to have a phone call with a tech person. It is not a " tech '' problem. I should not be speaking with any XXXX people because we are trying, as I stated above, to get out of XXXX.
